ogTitle: Flinton vs Flying Fish Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Flinton and Flying Fish Point.
heading: Flinton vs Flying Fish Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Flinton, Queensland and Flying Fish Point, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Flying Fish Point has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Flinton (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Flinton or Flying Fish Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Flinton has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Flying Fish Point scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Flinton scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Flying Fish Point scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Flinton or Flying Fish Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Flying Fish Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
